title: Xtra Crispy Ukrainian Wonton Skin Dumplings
description: A crispy Ukrainian dish featuring wonton skin, this recipe is a show-off dish that is sure to impress, with a grassy flavor profile and shojin-friendly ingredients. It uses mantecare-emulsify technique to create a unique taste experience.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine grated potato, chopped onion, and sliced mushroom.
In a separate bowl, whisk together soy milk and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Add the soy milk mixture to the potato mixture and stir until well combined.
Lay a wonton skin on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the potato mixture in the center of the skin.
Brush the edges of the skin with a little water and fold the skin into a triangle, pressing the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining wonton skins and filling.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the dumplings to the pan and fry until crispy and golden brown, about 3-4 minutes on each side.
Remove the dumplings from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
